content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: OISAIR PROJECT – TWO‑WAY CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON‑DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
@ Module name required by the task
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_3_nda_16

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1 – definition of the parties
type PartyRole = Disclosing(unit) | Receiving(unit)

type Party = {
    name : string,
    role : PartyRole
}

@ Clause 1 – definition of a piece of Confidential Information
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    id               : int,          -- stable identifier for the item
    owner            : Party,        -- the Disclosing Party that owns it
    retained_copy    : bool,         -- true if a copy may be kept for regulatory/insurance purposes
    returned         : bool,         -- true once the item has been returned to the Disclosing Party
    destroyed        : bool,         -- true once the item has been destroyed
    retention_allowed: bool          -- true if the retained‑copy exception is exercised
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the objects that will be aligned across modules
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties (the two contractual sides)
entity disclosing_party : Party
entity receiving_party : Party

@ Every piece of Confidential Information is modelled by an entity.
@ In a concrete deployment the solver will be given a collection of such
@ entities (e.g. by instantiating the module multiple times or by using a
@ list type).  The rules below are written so that they hold for *any*
@ ConfidentialInformation entity.
entity ci : ConfidentialInformation

@ Temporal flags required to express the post‑termination obligations
entity termination_occurred : bool          -- becomes true on the Termination Date
entity instruction_given    : bool          -- true when the Disclosing Party has issued the
                                          -- “return or destroy” instruction
entity days_since_instruction : int         -- number of days elapsed since the instruction

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Returns true when the allowed‑copy exception may be used (regulatory/insurance)
func allowed_retention(ci: ConfidentialInformation) -> bool:
    ci.retained_copy and ci.retention_allowed

@ Returns true when the item has been dealt with (returned or destroyed)
func dealt_with(ci: ConfidentialInformation) -> bool:
    ci.returned or ci.destroyed

@ Returns true when the deadline (7 days) has not been exceeded
func within_deadline(days: int) -> bool:
    days <= 7

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – normative requirements (deontic modality O = obligation)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 5 – obligation to return or destroy Confidential Information
rule O ci_return_or_destroy_after_termination:
    -- The obligation becomes active after the Termination Date and after the
    -- Disclosing Party has issued its written instruction.
    (termination_occurred and instruction_given and within_deadline(days_since_instruction))
        implies (dealt_with(ci) or allowed_retention(ci)) always

@ Clause 5 – the retained‑copy exception may be exercised only for one copy
rule O single_retained_copy_allowed:
    -- If a piece of information is marked as retained, the retention_allowed flag
    -- must be true for at most one piece per Receiving Party.
    let retained_cnt : int =
        case (receiving_party.role):
            | PartyRole.Receiving():
                -- The solver can count retained copies across all ci entities.
                -- In practice this is expressed by a separate alignment module;
                -- here we state the intended property.
                1   -- placeholder – the actual counting is performed by the solver
            | _: 0
    retained_cnt <= 1 always

@ Clause 5 – the Receiving Party must keep the information secret at all times
rule O confidentiality_obligation:
    -- This rule captures the general “do not disclose” duty.
    ci.owner.role = PartyRole.Disclosing() implies (ci.returned = false and ci.destroyed = false) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of module
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
